title: Dicalix poilanei
intro: Dicalix poilanei, also known as the Poilane's vine, is a species of plant in the family Menispermaceae, characterized by its woody vines and fragrant flowers. The health benefits of Dicalix poilanei include its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, wounds, and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of this plant include dimeric alkaloids, lignans, and other phenolic compounds, such as poilaneine and poilaneone, which have been shown to exhibit biological activity. Dicalix poilanei is commonly used in traditional medicine and is prepared into various herbal formulations, including infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are often used to treat respiratory and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Dicalix poilanei is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is native to Vietnam. The classification of Dicalix poilanei places it within the genus Dicalix. This plant is known to be a climbing shrub. Further classification includes its order as Gentianales and its kingdom as Plantae.
